title: Comprehensive mobile-first optimization with error handling and performance improvements

requirements.text: Add mobile-optimized viewport meta tags to frontend/index.html ensuring proper scaling and touch interactions (width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=5, user-scalable=yes, viewport-fit=cover)

quotes: Add viewport meta tags and mobile-specific CSS to ensure proper rendering

acceptanceCriteria: Viewport meta tag includes width=device-width and initial-scale=1
User can zoom up to 5x on mobile devices
Viewport covers safe areas on notched devices

requirements.text: Increase minimum touch target sizes to 44x44px for all interactive elements (buttons, links, controls) in mobile viewports using CSS media queries and Tailwind responsive utilities

quotes: Implement touch-friendly interactions with larger tap targets

acceptanceCriteria: All buttons and interactive elements are minimum 44x44px on mobile
Adequate spacing exists between adjacent interactive elements
Touch targets are visually clear and accessible

requirements.text: Implement mobile-specific error boundary component that wraps the entire application in frontend/src/App.tsx, displaying detailed error messages with component stack traces, recovery suggestions, and reload/clear storage buttons optimized for mobile screens

quotes: Add mobile-specific error boundaries with detailed error display

acceptanceCriteria: Error boundary catches all React errors during rendering
Error UI displays readable error messages on small screens
Users can reload the app or clear storage from the error screen
Error details include component stack trace

requirements.text: Add comprehensive console logging throughout frontend/src/main.tsx initialization sequence, logging each step: DOM ready, React mounting, QueryClient creation, InternetIdentityProvider initialization, and App component render, with timestamps and success/failure status

quotes: Console logging at each initialization step to identify where mobile loading fails

acceptanceCriteria: Each initialization step logs to console with timestamp
Success and failure states are clearly logged
Logs persist and are viewable in mobile browser dev tools
Critical errors are logged with full stack traces

requirements.text: Implement network connectivity check in frontend/src/App.tsx before attempting canister calls, displaying user-friendly offline message if network is unavailable, with retry button and automatic reconnection detection

quotes: Network connectivity checks before attempting canister calls

acceptanceCriteria: App checks network connectivity status on mount
Offline state displays clear message to user
Users can manually retry connection
App automatically detects when connection is restored

requirements.text: Add timeout handlers (30 second maximum) to all React Query queries and mutations in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts, displaying timeout error messages with actionable guidance when operations exceed the limit

quotes: Timeout handlers for long-running operations

acceptanceCriteria: All queries timeout after 30 seconds maximum
Timeout errors display user-friendly messages
Users receive guidance on how to resolve timeout issues
Timeout errors trigger proper cleanup of pending operations

requirements.text: Wrap all async route components in React Suspense boundaries with mobile-optimized loading states (skeleton screens or spinners) in frontend/src/App.tsx router configuration, ensuring graceful loading on slower mobile connections

quotes: React Suspense boundaries with mobile-optimized loading states

acceptanceCriteria: Each route has a Suspense boundary with fallback UI
Loading states are optimized for mobile screen sizes
Skeleton screens or spinners display during code splitting loads
Loading states do not block user interaction unnecessarily

requirements.text: Implement code splitting for all route components in frontend/src/App.tsx using React.lazy(), splitting FeedPage, ExplorePage, UploadPage, DashboardPage, and legal pages into separate chunks to reduce initial bundle size for mobile devices

quotes: Code splitting to reduce initial bundle size

acceptanceCriteria: Each major route is lazy-loaded as a separate chunk
Initial bundle size is reduced compared to current build
Route transitions load smoothly without blocking
Build output shows multiple chunk files

requirements.text: Add lazy loading to all images in frontend components using native loading='lazy' attribute, prioritizing above-the-fold images with loading='eager' to optimize bandwidth usage on mobile devices

quotes: Optimized images and assets for mobile bandwidth

acceptanceCriteria: All below-the-fold images use loading='lazy'
Critical above-the-fold images use loading='eager'
Images load progressively as user scrolls
Bandwidth usage is reduced on mobile connections

requirements.text: Implement debounced scroll and input handlers (300ms delay) in SearchBar component and any scroll-triggered components to reduce CPU usage and improve performance on mobile devices

quotes: Debounced scroll and interaction handlers

acceptanceCriteria: Search input is debounced by 300ms
Scroll handlers are debounced to reduce event frequency
Mobile scrolling remains smooth without jank
CPU usage is reduced during rapid interactions

requirements.text: Add proper cleanup and memory leak prevention to PlayerProvider context in frontend/src/player/PlayerProvider.tsx, ensuring audio elements are properly disposed when unmounted and event listeners are removed to prevent memory leaks on mobile devices with limited RAM

quotes: Memory leak prevention in audio components

acceptanceCriteria: Audio elements are properly cleaned up on unmount
Event listeners are removed when components unmount
No memory leaks occur during prolonged mobile usage
Audio context is properly closed when not in use

requirements.text: Replace the infinite loading spinner in frontend/index.html with a fallback error UI that appears after 15 seconds if the app hasn't loaded, displaying the specific error message, network status, and actionable recovery options (reload, clear cache, check connection)

quotes: Fallback UI that shows specific error messages instead of infinite loading spinner
User-facing error messages with actionable guidance

acceptanceCriteria: Loading spinner displays for maximum 15 seconds
Error UI appears if app fails to initialize within 15 seconds
Error message includes network connectivity status
Users can reload, clear cache, or check connection from error UI
Error screen is readable and functional on mobile devices

requirements.text: Add graceful degradation fallbacks throughout the application that allow core features (viewing feed, browsing explore page) to function even if certain features fail to load, displaying inline error messages for failed features while allowing the rest of the app to work

quotes: Graceful degradation if certain features fail to load

acceptanceCriteria: Feed page loads even if favorites query fails
Explore page functions if some category data fails to load
Failed features display inline error messages
Core navigation and browsing remain functional
Users can access available content despite partial failures

requirements.text: Optimize all CSS in frontend/src/index.css for mobile-first rendering by adding mobile-specific utility classes for touch interactions (active states, tap highlights), ensuring styles render efficiently on mobile browsers, and removing unused CSS

quotes: Add viewport meta tags and mobile-specific CSS to ensure proper rendering

acceptanceCriteria: Mobile-first CSS utilities are available for all components
Active states provide clear visual feedback on touch
Tap highlight colors are defined for interactive elements
Unused CSS is removed to reduce file size

requirements.text: Add comprehensive try-catch blocks with detailed error logging to all async operations in useActor hook (frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts), capturing actor initialization failures, canister communication errors, and authentication issues, logging them to console with actionable error messages

quotes: Enhanced error logging throughout the app lifecycle
Console logging at each initialization step to identify where mobile loading fails

acceptanceCriteria: All async actor operations are wrapped in try-catch
Actor initialization failures are logged with full details
Canister communication errors are caught and logged
Authentication errors display helpful messages
Error logs include timestamps and context

requirements.text: Implement progressive enhancement in AudioCard component (frontend/src/components/feed/AudioCard.tsx) so that basic audio playback works even if advanced features (favorites, replies) fail to load, with inline error messages for failed features

quotes: Graceful degradation if certain features fail to load

acceptanceCriteria: Audio playback functions independently of other features
Failed favorite/reply features display inline errors
Users can still play audio if mutations fail
Core functionality remains accessible on feature failures

requirements.text: Add mobile-specific touch event handlers to all interactive components (buttons, sliders, cards), implementing proper touch feedback (active states, ripple effects) and preventing default browser behaviors that interfere with custom interactions

quotes: Implement touch-friendly interactions with larger tap targets

acceptanceCriteria: All interactive elements respond to touch events
Touch feedback is visible and immediate
Default browser behaviors are prevented where appropriate
No conflicts between touch and click handlers
Touch interactions feel native and responsive

requirements.text: Optimize React Query cache configuration in frontend/src/main.tsx for mobile devices by reducing cache time, implementing staleTime appropriate for mobile connections, and adding retry logic with exponential backoff for failed queries

quotes: Performance improvements for mobile including code splitting, optimized images/assets for mobile bandwidth

acceptanceCriteria: Query cache times are optimized for mobile
Stale queries refetch at appropriate intervals
Failed queries retry with exponential backoff
Cache doesn't consume excessive mobile device memory

requirements.text: Add performance monitoring logging to frontend/src/main.tsx that tracks and logs key metrics (time to interactive, initial load time, bundle size, memory usage) to console on mobile devices, helping identify performance bottlenecks

quotes: Console logging at each initialization step to identify where mobile loading fails

acceptanceCriteria: Time to interactive is logged to console
Initial load time is measured and logged
Memory usage is tracked during app lifecycle
Performance metrics are mobile-specific
Logs help identify performance bottlenecks

requirements.text: Implement proper error recovery in Internet Identity authentication flow (frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts) with mobile-optimized error messages, automatic retry on transient failures, and clear guidance for authentication errors on mobile devices

quotes: Enhanced error logging throughout the app lifecycle
User-facing error messages with actionable guidance

acceptanceCriteria: Authentication errors display mobile-friendly messages
Transient failures trigger automatic retry
Users receive clear guidance on fixing auth issues
Error states don't block the entire application
Authentication flow degrades gracefully on mobile

constraints: Preserve all existing features including kids mode, favorites, audio replies, and content filtering
Maintain the single-actor backend architecture in backend/main.mo

Do not modify immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
Keep strict frontend stack: React, Shadcn, Tailwind, React Query
Ensure desktop functionality remains fully intact while optimizing for mobile
Do not implement real-time features, WebSockets, or external service integrations
Do not add third-party authentication providers beyond Internet Identity
nonGoals: Implementing new features beyond mobile optimization and error handling
Changing the visual design or layout beyond mobile responsiveness improvements
Adding service workers or PWA capabilities
Changing backend data models or API endpoints
Modifying the kids mode authentication system
Adding new navigation or page structures
